summary: The lymph from the tongue’s posterior part is primarily managed via specialized routes unrelated to central or marginal draining systems; it has its own distinctive path for lymph flow which differs significantly due to anatomical uniqueness compared to other regions such as anterior or lateral aspects. This distinction highlights why direct categorizations might miss this particular segment when discussing general lingual lymph node distributions.
